mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter financial conditions that raise discount rates and borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, it triggers a non-linear risk-off repricing as investors anticipate weaker consumption and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and retail sales signals a broad demand collapse that is worse than either signal alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both declining month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining INDPRO and RSAFS does not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than either proxy alone (F-test p > 0.05 for interaction term), or positive returns occur in more than 45% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy, as indicated by a federal funds rate above 5 percent, a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL accelerates fears of further tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases do not correlate with negative 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC above -0.02 over a 2-year rolling window), or the conditional mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-side deterioration that compresses corporate profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean conditional return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS do not predict positive 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window), or the mean forward return during positive payroll months is not greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut of 25 basis points or more within a single month signals a reactive easing cycle that reduces short-term borrowing costs and triggers a rapid reflation trade, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of FEDFUNDS month-over-month decline of at least 0.25 percentage points are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean 5-day return is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in housing starts and retail sales reflects a dual contraction in durable goods and consumer spending that amplifies recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both declining month-over-month
failure_condition: The combination of declining HOUST and RSAFS does not generate more negative 60-day SPX returns than a simple linear combination of the two series (interaction t-stat above -1.5), or positive returns occur in more than 40% of joint-trigger instances.
